title: Fact-resolution application result
description: Immutable record that one signed fact-resolution decision was applied exactly once (DEC-0016). Every identity is deterministic (algorithm fact-resolution-id-sha256-1.0.0, semantic layer recomputes): artifact_id is 'fra' + sha256 hex over the canonical JSON of {decision_digest, receipt_ref}; each losing successor is 'crs' + sha256 over {application_ref, losing_claim_ref}; the after-snapshot and after-analysis are 'fsn'/'fan' + sha256 over {application_ref, role}. Deterministic identities plus the receipt-sourced consumed_at timestamp make application idempotent and crash-recoverable: a retry of the same operation recomputes byte-identical artifacts, resumes only missing writes, refuses conflicting content, and a replay after completion returns this stored record without creating anything. The record binds the consumed approval (evidence reference, signed payload digest, receipt, key, policy, and registry), the before-state (the signed decision and its snapshot/analysis), the created losing-claim revisions, and the after-state (fresh snapshot and analysis in which the resolved contradiction is closed). Application creates immutable successor revisions only — no stored claim is ever overwritten, edited, deleted, or auto-upgraded; the winner is not mutated. Single-host/single-writer file atomicity only; no distributed-transaction claim is made.

application_algorithm.description: versioned derivation algorithm for the application, successor, and after-artifact identities; a future change is a new version, never a silent reinterpretation

decision_ref.description: artifact_id of the signed fact-resolution-decision this application executed

decision_digest.description: canonical content digest of the stored decision; equals the signed target_artifact_digest of the consumed approval

evidence_ref.description: evidence_id of the consumed approval evidence (unauthenticated label; the cryptographic link is payload_digest)

payload_digest.description: domain-separated canonical digest of the signed approval payload (approval-payload-sha256-1.0.0); matches the immutable receipt

receipt_ref.description: receipt_id of the atomic single-use consumption this application executed under; the application identity is derived from it

fact_key.description: copied from the decision for audit discoverability

contradiction_fingerprint.description: copied from the decision for audit discoverability

before_snapshot_ref.description: the decision's claim_snapshot_ref: the pre-resolution snapshot, stale after application by construction (DEC-0013)

before_analysis_ref.description: the decision's truth_analysis_ref: the pre-resolution analysis, stale after application by construction

created_losing_revisions.description: one entry per losing claim, sorted by losing_claim_ref in code-unit order (semantic layer); each successor supersedes the losing head, preserves its content, sets verification_status to contradicted, and records resolution_decision_ref

successor_claim_ref.description: deterministic successor identity (semantic layer recomputes over {application_ref, losing_claim_ref})

successor_content_digest.description: canonical content digest of the created successor revision; recovery validates any existing successor against this expected content

after_snapshot_ref.description: deterministic identity of the fresh post-resolution snapshot (semantic layer recomputes)

after_analysis_ref.description: deterministic identity of the fresh post-resolution analysis in which the resolved contradiction is closed (semantic layer recomputes)

after_claim_set_digest.description: the fresh snapshot's aggregate claim-set digest: the canonical namespace state this application produced

status.description: pinned: only completed applications persist a result; failed or partial attempts persist nothing at this address and are resumed by retry

consumed_at.description: the receipt's consumption timestamp; every artifact this application created carries it, so retries recompute byte-identical content
